title: Lace executor configuration (lace.config)
description: TOML structure for `lace.config` files. Conforms to lace-spec.md §11. Consumers parse the TOML, then validate the resulting JSON against this schema. All sections are optional; the executor uses defaults for absent fields.
Top-level keys: `executor`, `result`, `extensions`, plus environment-suffixed `lace.config.{env}` overrides (handled by the loader, not represented in this schema).

extensions.description: Per-extension configuration. Keys are extension names (matching .laceext [extension].name). Each value is a free-form TOML table — extension-defined.
Extension names to load at startup. Each must have a corresponding [extensions.{name}] section pointing at its .laceext file. Default: [] (no extensions active).

maxRedirects.description: System redirect limit. Validator rejects scripts where redirects.max exceeds this.

maxTimeoutMs.description: System timeout limit in ms (default 5 min). Validator rejects timeout.ms exceeding this.

user_agent.description: Override the default outgoing User-Agent header (spec §3.6). When omitted, the executor sends `lace-probe/<executor-version> (<implementation-name>)`. Script-level headers still take precedence per request.

path.description: Where to save the result JSON. Directory path: timestamped filename auto-generated. Full file path: overwrites that file. `false`: do not save.

dir.description: Directory for request/response body files. Defaults to the same directory as result.path.

ExtensionConfig.description: Per-extension config overrides. The `laceext` key is reserved for the extension file path; all other keys override the extension's `.config` defaults (see lace-extensions.md §2.3). Keys absent here retain the extension's declared defaults.

laceext.description: Path to the .laceext file or `builtin:{name}` for bundled extensions.
